methodsAttempted.query: site:tu-dortmund.de TU Dortmund KI ChatGPT kuenstliche Intelligenz Lehre Pruefung Studierende Richtlinie
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the public TU Dortmund ChatGPT teaching handout PDF, a TU Dortmund news page about AI in teaching, a digital study and teaching strategy PDF, a Campus-KI announcement, and a podcast item.
Firecrawl search confirmed the same public handout PDF and strategy PDF, plus a faculty-level declaration PDF that was not used for university-wide claims.
The public Digitale Lehre ChatGPT page links the German and English TU Dortmund ChatGPT handouts and points to ServicePortal wording suggestions for declarations.

methodsAttempted.query: official TU Dortmund ServicePortal KI Campus-KI Academic Cloud
methodsAttempted.notes: ServicePortal AI resources and Campus-KI direct access were checked with Firecrawl; ServicePortal redirected to SSO and Campus-KI exposed an organization-selection login flow, so neither was used as direct policy evidence.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found public TU Dortmund guidance and strategy evidence on tu-dortmund.de and digitale-lehre.tu-dortmund.de. Claims are limited to the public ChatGPT guidance hub, the October 2023 TU Dortmund handout PDF, the January 2025 Campus-KI announcement, and the 2025 digital study and teaching strategy PDF. Login-only ServicePortal pages and Campus-KI application access were documented but not used as evidence.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Zum Umgang mit ChatGPT in der Lehre - eine Aktualisierung der ersten Handreichung der TU Dortmund
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Wie eingangs schon skizziert, ist es eine universitaere Aufgabe, nicht zuletzt technische Entwicklungen voranzutreiben und kritisch zu begleiten. KI-Tools sind und werden in vielen Lebensbereichen zunehmend als alltaegliche Hilfsmittel eingestuft und genutzt.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The handout frames AI tools as increasingly everyday aids and says universities should advance and critically accompany technical developments.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section: Warum ist es nicht zielfuehrend, ChatGPT in der Lehre zu verbieten?

artifacts.sourceTitle: Zum Umgang mit ChatGPT in der Lehre - eine Aktualisierung der ersten Handreichung der TU Dortmund
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Vor diesem Hintergrund ist davon abzuraten, KI-Tools zur Kontrolle von Pruefungsleistungen zu nutzen bzw. grundsaetzlich urheberrechtlich geschuetzte Werke dritter Personen hochzuladen. Werden im Rahmen der Nutzung von KI-Tools personenbezogene Daten wie Namen der Studierenden oder Matrikelnummern mit hochgeladen, werden Vorgaben der Datenschutz-Grundverordnung missachtet.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The handout advises against using AI tools to check examination work and warns about uploading personal data such as names or matriculation numbers.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow claim about the existence and audience of the public ChatGPT orientation page.
Approved after scope check: the claim is limited to the handout text about AI tools as everyday aids and the university role in critically accompanying technical developments.
Approved as guidance about possible subject-specific declaration supplementation, avoiding a universal mandate.
Approved because the evidence directly warns against using AI tools for checking examination work and identifies GDPR concerns for personal data.
Approved from the official TU Dortmund announcement; login-only Campus-KI application content was not used as evidence.
Approved as a strategy-level claim about listed goals in the official digitalization strategy.

artifacts.title: TU Dortmund University staged AI policy evidence
artifacts.summary: TU Dortmund public sources provide evidence for a ChatGPT teaching guidance hub, a German handout framing AI tools as increasingly everyday aids while also advising transparency around AI-tool use in assessments, a privacy/copyright caution for uploading examination work or personal data to AI tools, a 2025 Campus-KI access announcement, and an AI action field in the digital study and teaching strategy.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
ServicePortal AI-resource and examination-declaration pages were login-gated and were not used as claim evidence.
The direct Campus-KI service URL led to an organization-selection or SSO flow; the public TU Dortmund announcement was used instead.
Claims are staged candidates only and are not marked canonical or human reviewed.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
